voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: Fun story, this song is actually a cover. The original writer decided to toss his original lyrics and take it to Rob Thomas. Rob Thomas rewrote the melody and lyrics, giving it the name Smooth. Thomas took it to Santana, thinking he would be the best man to bring the song to life. Santana thought Thomas should do the vocals. So, while it's labeled as Santana featuring Rob Thomas, it's really Rob Thomas featuring Santana.

The jury originally found in favor of LZ in 2016 but lost on an appeal. Apparently, the jury was not allowed to listen to the other song (Taurus by Spirit) to decide if it was indeed a ripoff, which the appellate court thought was unfair. LZ then won an appeal of that appeal and the supreme court declined to take up the case in 2020, effectively ending the lawsuit in LZ's favor.

voters.comment: Don't make me point to the sign! (Wayne's World reference)I didn't remember if they ever resolved the lawsuit so I had to look it up. The jury originally found in favor of LZ in 2016 but lost on an appeal. Apparently, the jury was not allowed to listen to the other song (Taurus by Spirit) to decide if it was indeed a ripoff, which the appellate court thought was unfair. LZ then won an appeal of that appeal and the supreme court declined to take up the case in 2020, effectively ending the lawsuit in LZ's favor.

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: Fun story, this song is actually a cover. The original writer decided to toss his original lyrics and take it to Rob Thomas. Rob Thomas rewrote the melody and lyrics, giving it the name Smooth. Thomas took it to Santana, thinking he would be the best man to bring the song to life. Santana thought Thomas should do the vocals. So, while it's labeled as Santana featuring Rob Thomas, it's really Rob Thomas featuring Santana.

The devil lets the singer know that it's fine to take time to yourself to take of yourself and not worry about others. Then, the singer throws it back on the devil when the devil complains about being overworked and too tired. The devil responds by saying that the singer wants everyone to need them or they would let it got or at least accept help. Finally, the singer resolves to just let everything go.

Back then I wanted to be a cool kid who only listened to cool bands BUT then I grew up and realized you can like BOTH wildly popular Taylor Swift AND "cool bands" at the same time. That's why I said I was torn to even submit it as a guilty pleasure because I don't care as much anymore.I could even say I do like a lot of her slower songs better than her hits, so it's more of a guilty pleasure to be jamming out to one of her poppy hits like this one.

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: Back when I worked at a summer tech camp, I traveled to locations all over the country. One spring session, me and my one coworker used to sneak up on each other and surprise each other by playing this song at full blast. No idea why but it became our own personal Rickroll. Summer started, he went to Florida, I went to California and a few months went by. The last week of camp, I was sitting quietly working on my laptop and all of a sudden, my internet browser started acting weird and any time I opened a new tab, it redirected to a YouTube video of this damn song. WTF! I sat there staring at my screen in disbelief as this dude who was supposed to be working on the other side of the country came running around the corner with the biggest shit eating grin on his face, saying nothing but the two words: "you're welcome." I've never been pranked so hard in my life and I hate that you're making me give points to this fucking song!!

What were you doing Robert? Leaving lion cages open? What the hell man, maybe your wife should kill you.

voters.name: Amanda
voters.comment: My mom had our house exorcised and a cross put in the wall when I was a kid. Jess claimed she saw a lady in white robes and white hair standing at the foot of her bed. On later seeing photos of my mom's mom she said that was her in her hospital gowns after she was institutionalized. I never met her, she died before I was born of a heart attack and Jessica met her once or twice I think. I figure now Jess was just fucking with me like her and her childhood friend performing seances. But my dad's girlfriend now says she senses a presence all these years later. And my dad is asking me about the house and my experiences. We all had some odd experiences not all at that house after my Mom passed. Grayson's daycare provider said he  was talking to Nonnie but no one was there when Nicole called her. To say she would be late.My mom was en route to the ER at that momen.t I  attribute it complicated grief mental condition 😅  😆 So this is probably my Dad. We are probably those neighbors.

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: Oh my god! I just saw a video dunking on some moron for using this album's cover to claim that Supertramp knew about 9/11. Yes. They claimed that. Apparently, the U and P in Super look like 9 11 when flipped backwards and is clearly a plane over NYC, and there's only ever been one time that planes were over NYC. The comments pointed out all the reasons this is ridiculous, though one comment on the original post claimed that the drink the waitress is holding means that the Jews did 9/11 (because I guess they're assuming it's juice?). Someone in the original post commented that they should listen to The Logical Song. To be fair, that's just good advice to listen to a good song.*Ahem* As a ska boy, I truly believe that more songs need brass instruments. This may be a tuba, which is very limited in it's use, but thumbs up.

Very good. VERY VERY good, but since everyone has had at least one person corner them and berate them non-stop about how transcendent they are, it leads to an overall "eh" feeling when you don't like them as much as the zealots.

voters.comment: This isn't the Chuck Barry I would have chosen but doing some digging during my insomnia the Beach Boys Surfing USA Brian Wilson admitted the music from Chuck Barry's Sweet Little Sixteen with new lyrics. So basically they pulled an Elvis which was sadly common at the time especially with segregation. So, I feel this deserves more points to honor Chuck Berry's memory. https://youtu.be/ZLV4NGpoy_E?si=dsP31qyQDcspvwGX Also, speaking of apparently Elvis has beach music, I haven't listened to yet. He also did Surfin movies. That was new information for me, but maybe just me. Also, this video below calls out about Fun, Fun, Fun riff   which could get into a larger conversation about where the line is between inspiration and straight out theft. But taking a whole instrumental with no credit is so unethical. And Chuck got his dues and credits in the end through the courts.https://youtube.com/shorts/j0Uk8_lMTnw?si=xusHsRb9-a0zoFcshttps://youtu.be/YcDRS4xexJc?si=x2NVD03lHgZqt0_0

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: Bittersweet story time. I actually always kinda hated this song, but everyone in my family seemed to love it. Then a few years ago when my uncle got sick with cancer and was bedridden, we would all sing songs with him to pass the time and lift his spirits. This one easily being his favorite, it made it into the rotation pretty much every time anyone visited him. When he eventually passed away and we were laying him to rest, someone started singing this and before long we had a group of 50 something odd people all belting out Country Roads in the middle of the cemetery. Weirdest funeral I've ever been to! And while I'm still not a huge fan of the song itself, it always makes me smile and think about him whenever I hear it.

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: I still think the best thing that P!atD did was to change their sound. This song is good and memorable, but there's no arguing that they only made it because of Fall Out Boy. When the other members left, or he kicked them out... I'm not sure what happened to them, and he changed to a much more pop rock sound, the music got much better and more unique. So much so that, when Fall Out Boy came back from their hiatus, they ripped off what he was doing at the time to be their new sound. Call me a hipster, but I prefer that run of Take This to Your Grave to Infinity On High over ANY song they've put out since.

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: In the war, I side with Backstreet over *NSYNC. Fun story, NSYNC was the first concert I ever went to. My sister was a huge fan and they were putting on a concert in Hershey. My parents took us boys to Hershey Park, where I ended up developing my fear of heights (Man, this trip was weirdly impactful on my life...), and then we went to the concert after. It was shortly before the release of their album Celebrity. I only remember two things about that concert: Pop was pretty catchy and fun to watch performed live and near my dad, brother, and myself (because my mom and sister got better seats to accommodate my sisters handicap) there was a buxom woman who was not shy about putting her um... natural gifts... on display in a crowd that included a then 9 year old me. I remember the feeling being more of shock at her lack of modesty than any kind of arousal. Anyway, this is a catchy one but Backstreet 4-ever.
